name: Santuario del Brezo Loop
name_original: Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Brezo – circular desde Respenda de la Peña
description: This difficult 19.5-mile touring cycling route from Respenda de la Peña climbs to the Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Brezo.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Respenda De La Peña is characterized by its mountainous landscape, situated at approximately 1,015 meters (3,330 feet) above sea level. The region serves as a gateway to the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, featuring significant peaks, river valleys from the Carrión and Pisuerga rivers, and extensive forests of birch, beech, oak, and holm oak. The Sierra del Brezo mountain range further contributes to the area's varied terrain, offering routes with notable elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Respenda De La Peña The most popular touring cycling route is Santuario del Brezo Loop, a 19.5 miles (31.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 4 minutes to complete. This route ascends to the historic Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Brezo, offering both physical challenge and cultural interest. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puerto de Picones (1316 m) – Velilla del Río Carrión loop from Guardo Apeadero, a difficult 30.7 miles (49.3 km) path. This route features substantial elevation changes through the mountainous terrain, providing a demanding experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Guardo – Carrión River in Guardo loop from Villalba de Guardo, a 19.5 miles (31.3 km) trail leading through the Carrión River valley, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Respenda De La Peña is defined by mountainous terrain, scenic river valleys, and dense forests, with routes varying in difficulty and distance. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Respenda De La Peña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Respenda De La Peña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Respenda De La Peña?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes around Respenda De La Peña, offering a variety of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Respenda De La Peña?
A: The region around Respenda De La Peña is characterized by its mountainous landscape, situated at approximately 1,015 meters (3,330 feet) above sea level. You can expect a rugged landscape of hills and valleys, with significant elevation changes, especially in areas like the Sierra del Brezo mountain range. Routes often feature ascents and descents, providing a challenging yet rewarding experience.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While the area is known for its mountainous terrain, there are routes of varying difficulty. Out of the 12 available routes, 7 are classified as moderate, offering less strenuous options compared to the more difficult ascents. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170996065/santuario-de-nuestra-senora-del-brezo-circular-desde-castrejon-de-la-pena-por-la-cuenca-minera-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Brezo – circular desde Castrejón de la Peña por la cuenca minera palentina&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that still offers scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Respenda De La Peña?
A: Given its mountainous and high-altitude location, the spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling. Summers can be warm, and winters may bring colder temperatures and snow, especially at higher elevations. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1171002845/santuario-del-brezo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Brezo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to the historic Sanctuary of Our Lady of Brezo. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Picones (1316 m) – Velilla del Río Carrión loop from Guardo Apeadero&lt;/a&gt;, offering substantial elevation changes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while touring cycling around Respenda De La Peña?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and cultural landmarks. You can explore the Montaña Palentina Natural Park with its majestic peaks, deep valleys, and lush forests. Notable features include the Carrión and Pisuerga rivers. Culturally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5649404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Brezo&lt;/a&gt; is a significant 13th-century site at 1,400 meters altitude. You might also pass through settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5543399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo&lt;/a&gt;, situated on the Carrión River.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with cultural or historical exploration in Respenda De La Peña?
A: Absolutely. The northern Palencia region, including Respenda De La Peña, is renowned for its Romanesque art. Many touring cycling routes can be planned to include visits to well-preserved medieval buildings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5649404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Brezo&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering both a physical challenge and a historical destination.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Respenda De La Peña?
A: The touring cycling routes in Respenda De La Peña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning natural backdrops and the blend of challenging ascents with scenic river valleys.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Respenda De La Peña?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation changes, some of the moderate routes might be suitable for families with older children or those with good cycling experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24567278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo – Carrión River in Guardo loop from Villalba de Guardo&lt;/a&gt; follows the Carrión River valley and is classified as moderate, potentially offering a more accessible experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Respenda De La Peña?
A: Specific parking information for each trailhead is best found on the individual route pages on komoot. However, municipalities like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5543399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo&lt;/a&gt;, often serve as convenient starting points for exploring the Montaña Palentina Natural Park and may offer parking facilities.

Q: Are there options for food and drink along the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes or pubs directly on every route may vary, towns and villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Respenda de la Peña&lt;/a&gt; itself and nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5543399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo&lt;/a&gt; offer amenities where you can find places to eat and drink. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote routes.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available?
A: One of the longer and more demanding routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31875875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrión River in Guardo – Guardo loop from Guardo Apeadero&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 30.8 kilometers (19.1 miles) and is classified as moderate. For a more challenging long ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Picones (1316 m) – Velilla del Río Carrión loop from Guardo Apeadero&lt;/a&gt;, at 49.3 kilometers (30.7 miles).
tips: The Sanctuary of the Virgen del Brezo, located on the Palencia Mountain, is a temple of great devotion and the scene of a popular pilgrimage. At an altitude of 1,400 m, in the Sierra del Brezo, the sanctuary houses the Romanesque carving of the Virgin of Brezo. Built in the 13th century and renovated in the 18th century, the sanctuary has a church, chapel and guest house, and offers peace and natural beauty to its visitors.

Small town located in a valley, with the Sierra del Brezo as a background and close to the Palencia Mountain. Surrounded by impressive natural landscapes, in this quiet town the Church of the Immaculate Conception stands out above its houses.

faqs.question: How challenging is the touring cycling route to Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Brezo?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult for touring cyclists. It features a demanding ascent to the Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Brezo, known for its 'explosive' nature with sections reaching gradients of 15% to 16%. The climb averages over 7.1% over 3.8 kilometers, making it particularly challenging, especially if carrying luggage.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the circular route from Respenda de la Peña?
faqs.answer: The route primarily traverses the Montaña Palentina region, characterized by significant elevation changes. While the area boasts well-preserved and little-traveled roads, a circular route might incorporate some unpaved sections if it deviates from main roads, adding to the challenge for traditional touring bicycles. Expect varied surfaces and considerable climbing and descending.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 31.3 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 4 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Santuario de Nuestra Señora del Brezo, an 18th-century neoclassical Marian shrine nestled in the Sierra del Brezo. You will also pass by the Church of the Immaculate Conception in Respenda de la Peña. The entire route offers breathtaking high mountain landscapes within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park.

faqs.question: Where does the circular route start and is there parking available in Respenda de la Peña?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting and ending in Respenda de la Peña. While specific parking details are not provided, Respenda de la Peña is a small village, and roadside parking or designated areas for visitors are usually available near the village center or the Church of the Immaculate Conception.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the natural surroundings are generally welcoming, specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural park areas and near livestock, and ensure they are fit enough for the challenging terrain and distance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montaña Palentina region is best enjoyed during the warmer months, typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). This avoids the harsh winter conditions and ensures more pleasant cycling weather. The Santuario holds a major annual pilgrimage on September 21st, which might affect crowds if you choose to visit around that time.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in the Montaña Palentina Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads and trails within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Olvidado (Matamorisca-Puente Almuhey), the [CIMA PA02] Santuario del Brezo, and smaller local paths like Calle Camino de Respenda and Senda de la cruz.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the route in Respenda de la Peña?
faqs.answer: Respenda de la Peña is a small village, and while specific establishments are not detailed, it's common for such villages in rural Spain to have a local bar or guesthouse. For more options, consider nearby larger towns. The Santuario itself has a guesthouse as part of its complex, though availability should be checked in advance.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning high mountain landscapes within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. The region is known for its vast natural beauty, including peaks exceeding 2,000 meters, and the abundant heather (brezo) on the mountain slopes. Views from the Santuario encompass surrounding mountains and plains, offering an immersive experience amidst diverse flora and fauna.
